name: Dutch Pancakes with Spicy Apples
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp cornstarch; mixed with 2 tbsp cold water
3 medium red apples, cored and sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees.
2. Spray a 10-inch round oven-safe skillet with nonstick cooking spray.
3. Beat the eggs in a bowl.
4. Mix in the flour, granulated sugar, and milk until smooth.
5. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and bake for 15 minutes.
6. Reduce the heat to 325 degrees.
7. Bake for an additional 40-45 minutes, or until set and golden brown.
8. Remove from oven immediately and cut into wedges.
9. Top with spicy apples.
10. For the spicy apple topping, melt margarine in a skillet over medium heat.
11. Stir together cornstarch mixture (cornstarch mixed with cold water), brown sugar, and cinnamon until thickened.
12. Add the sliced apples to the skillet and cook until tender but still crisp, stirring occasionally.
13. Spoon the cooked apples over the hot pancakes.
14. Combine light sour cream and powdered sugar in a small bowl.
15. Dollop a spoonful of the sour cream mixture onto each serving.
16. Garnish with pansies or dianthus if desired.
17. Serve alongside sausage.
